title: Syrian Chocolate Fritters
description: A pillowy, adventurous twist on traditional fritters featuring rich, plain chocolate and a paleo-friendly pasta-water emulsion. Perfect for snacking or dessert.
instructions: Whisk together almond flour, unsweetened cocoa powder, coconut sugar, and salt in a large bowl.
In a separate bowl, whisk together egg, pasta cooking water, coconut oil, vanilla extract, and xanthan gum.
Add honey and whisk until well combined.
Add chocolate chips and stir until they are evenly coated with the wet ingredients.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until a thick batter forms.
Let the batter rest for 10 minutes to allow the starches to absorb.
Using a small cookie scoop, drop the batter onto a parchment-lined baking sheet, leaving about 2 inches of space between each fritter.
Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for 15-20 minutes, or until the fritters are firm to the touch and the edges are lightly browned.
Allow the fritters to cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before transferring them to a wire rack to cool completely.
Serve warm or at room temperature, dust with powdered sugar if desired.
